caption: This educational presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" focuses on "Tutorial – 2," specifically calculating the "Work/unit mass of gas compressed." The slide features a thermodynamic graph plotting temperature (T) against entropy (s), showing processes of constant temperature ("T=const"), constant entropy ("s=const"), and constant pressure ("p=const"). A data table provides values for pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s) for states 1, 2, and f. A mathematical formula for work, $W/m = T_1(s_1 - s_2) - (h_1 - h_2)$, is presented, followed by a calculated result: "W/m = 300(4.4 - 2.75) - (462 - 430) = 463 J/g". The bottom of the slide attributes the content to "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" and includes an "NPTEL" logo. Across the frames, a red circle moves along the final calculation line, highlighting different parts of the equation.
